text.title: Difficult Conversations Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding difficult-conversations. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The script MUST separate observable facts (FACT field) from the author's interpretation (STORY field). Mixing them is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Stories drive feelings; conflating them prevents the listener from engaging with reality.

rule.statement: The script MUST include a 'safety check' cue (watch for silence: sarcasm/withdrawal; or violence: cutting off/labelling). When triggered, pause content and restore safety first.
rule.rationale: Content cannot land while the other person is in fight-or-flight; safety restoration must precede content.

rule.statement: A STATE opening MUST fit in 3-5 sentences (under 60s spoken). Anything longer reads as monologue.
rule.rationale: Long openings destroy the safety being built; brevity invites response.

rule.statement: DESC format MUST be used only in async/written contexts (Slack, email). Reading DESC aloud as a script is forbidden.
rule.rationale: DESC is calibrated for async delivery; spoken aloud it sounds prepared and clinical.

rule.statement: Every conversation MUST close with WHO does WHAT by WHEN + follow-up date. Vague commitment ('we'll do better') is forbidden as the close.
rule.rationale: Without specific accountability both parties leave with different interpretations; follow-up date closes the loop.
